behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a local module boundary: diff parsing/hunk extraction must hand off correctly to prompt/input assembly. A single-function unit test can miss wiring/state mistakes between modules. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; fixtures with synthetic diffs are sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this behavior is about input scoping before model judgment, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real in-process pipeline that converts a PR patch into review input using multi-file unified-diff fixtures; assert only changed hunks (with minimal context) are included and unchanged file bodies are excluded.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics and API contract behavior (path/commit/line vs position mapping). Mocks can hide real placement failures (MOCK-HIDE + REAL-INFRA). Rejected Integration because in-process stubs cannot faithfully reproduce GitHub’s server-side diff anchoring behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed anchors, then fetch review comments from GitHub and verify each appears on the intended changed line/path in the diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on actual model judgment quality (LLM-DEP). Deterministic stubs always pass/fail by fixture choice and cannot validate defect-detection capability. Rejected Workflow because behavior asks for one detection outcome, not multi-agentic-stage sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live model on a curated PR diff containing a seeded bug; assert at least one returned finding correctly targets the seeded defect class/location.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is state handoff/invariant checking across local components (comment-post response collection -> summary builder). Real network is not required to catch the core failure if API responses are stubbed consistently. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on same-run ID linkage, not external API edge behavior. Rejected Agentic because mapping correctness is independent of model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting-orchestration modules with stubbed inline-post responses returning comment IDs; build summary payload and assert every summary item references an ID emitted in that run and no foreign IDs are present.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when findings flow through multiple local stages (collection, dedupe/filter, serialization). This is module wiring/state propagation, not single-function logic in most implementations. Rejected System because dropping can be detected without real HTTP; rejected Agentic because this checks preservation of produced findings, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a multi-finding set through the real in-process aggregation and summary-construction path; assert cardinality/content preservation from inline findings list to final summary payload.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real PR and end-to-end API interactions (read diff + post comments), so real HTTP/external contract is on the failure boundary (REAL-INFRA). Rejected Agentic because findings are fixed precomputed (no dependence on model output). Rejected Workflow because no multi-agentic sequence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full automation against a real repository PR using a fixed findings fixture; verify diff retrieval, successful inline comment creation, and successful top-level summary post in one run.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end agent pipeline combining real infrastructure and model-driven analysis across sequential stages; correctness depends on both agentic decision(s) and downstream orchestration (MULTI-STEP/full pipeline). Rejected Agentic because single-step model validation would not catch cross-stage failures in posting/summarization after live generation. Rejected System because system-only tests with fixed findings miss model-dependent failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute unattended live run on a real PR: fetch diff, generate findings with live model, post inline comments, then post summary; assert pipeline completion plus consistency between generated findings, posted inline comments, and summary content.
